nativeRange.habitat: Rocky and rubble substrate in shallow to subtidal reef zones; photic zone

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product photo from Mosaic Macros listing — sole hobby source for this species

summary: Rare calcareous red macroalgae (family Galaxauraceae) with a branching, fuzzy thallus reaching only a few centimetres. Rarely encountered in the aquarium trade and generally considered an advanced-care specimen; stable calcium and alkalinity are essential.
careNotes: Bramble Thicket is a calcareous red macroalgae in the genus Galaxaura (family Galaxauraceae, order Nemaliales), distinguished by fine filaments along its reddish-pink branching thallus that give it a fuzzy or bristly appearance reminiscent of brambles. It typically stays compact, reaching roughly 5–8 cm at most, making it one of the smaller display macroalgae available to reef keepers. As a calcified red alga, it deposits calcium carbonate into its thallus — the branching body of the plant — and is therefore likely to benefit from stable calcium (400–450 ppm) and magnesium (1250–1350 ppm) levels consistent with a well-maintained reef system. Because species-specific husbandry data are limited, these targets are extrapolated from family-level care of related calcareous macroalgae such as Haliptilon, Jania, and Corallina; individual results may vary. Standard reef water parameters — SG 1.023–1.026, temperature 24–27°C, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–12 dKH — are advised. Medium light intensity is generally recommended for calcareous red algae; intense lighting has been reported to bleach calcified thalli in some reef systems. Growth rate tends to be slow, which is typical for macroalgae with the metabolic demands of skeletal calcification. Propagation in the aquarium is typically achieved by fragmentation of the branching thallus, with pieces secured to rockwork by wedging, zip-tie, fishing line, or aquarium-safe superglue. Bramble Thicket is rarely encountered in the aquarium trade and is available primarily through specialty macroalgae retailers as a display specimen, sold on a plug or as a larger cutting. A closely related species, Galaxaura rugosa (sold in the trade as 'Tubular Thicket'), shares a similar calcified morphology and broadly comparable reef husbandry requirements. IMPORTANT: as a red alga, Bramble Thicket will MELT at its base if buried in or resting on sand — it must be attached to rockwork rather than placed on the substrate.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Confirmed genus (Galaxaura sp.), trade name, morphology (fuzzy branching thallus, reddish-pink, max ~2 in). Minimal husbandry parameters on product page.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Galaxaura
additional.notes: Confirmed family Galaxauraceae, order Nemaliales, class Florideophyceae (Rhodophyta). Cosmopolitan marine distribution. Used for taxonomy and distribution only — no aquarium husbandry data.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
